Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Looking at the event log Case 1 RA VF AE RMI VF AD SC AC Case 2 RA VF AE AD SC AC Case 3 RA VF AE RMI RMI VF AD SC AC Key observations 1 All cases start with RA 2 All cases have VF at least once 3 All cases have AE 4 RMI is optional some cases have it 1 and 3 some do not 2 5 When RMI occurs VF can occur again re validation 6 Case 3 shows RMI can happen multiple times twice 7 After the validation phase AD happens 8 SC and AC happen at the end in all cases

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Process flow analysis RA is the first activity Then VF happens Then AE happens There is a loop RMI can happen and if it does VF can happen again After the loop exits AD happens Then SC happens Then AC happens end

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So the structure should be 1 RA sequence 2 VF sequence 3 AE sequence 4 Loop containing RMI and re validation of VF 5 AD sequence 6 SC sequence 7 AC sequence

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Actually looking more carefully at the log Case 1 RMI VF one RMI then re validation Case 3 RMI RMI VF two RMI then re validation

Name: Backtracking - PE

Text: A B means 1 Execute A 2 Either exit OR execute B then A again So RMI VF would mean Execute RMI Either exit OR execute VF then RMI again

Text: This matches the observed behavior where RMI can happen multiple times and after RMI VF can happen

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This means either Execute the loop RMI can happen with re validation VF Execute nothing tau skip the loop entirely

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE

Name: Validation - NE
Text: The loop RMI VF allows RMI to happen once then exit RMI to happen then VF then RMI again multiple times This matches Case 1 RMI VF and Case 3 RMI RMI VF
